title: US-Iran Talks Rescued After Arab Lobbying
detail: Plans for US-Iran nuclear talks nearly collapsed after Iran insisted on moving the venue from Istanbul to Oman and limiting the agenda to nuclear issues only. At least nine Middle Eastern countries urgently lobbied the Trump administration not to cancel, with leaders reaching out 'at the highest levels.' The White House agreed to proceed 'to be respectful' to regional allies and to continue pursuing the diplomatic track. Talks were rescheduled for February 6 in Muscat.

title: Trump Signs Executive Order on Iran Maximum Pressure
detail: President Trump signed an executive order authorizing up to 25% tariffs on countries conducting trade with Iran, as part of a broader maximum pressure campaign. The order directs the Treasury secretary to impose maximum economic pressure including driving Iran's oil exports to zero, while the State Department was ordered to lead a diplomatic campaign to isolate Tehran. Trump described the measures as 'very tough' but said he remained open to a deal.

title: Witkoff Meets Netanyahu Ahead of Oman Talks
detail: White House special envoy Steve Witkoff met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and senior Israeli defense officials in Jerusalem to coordinate positions ahead of the Iran nuclear talks in Muscat. Israeli officials briefed Witkoff on Israel's latest intelligence regarding Iran's nuclear program and the ongoing fortification of the Isfahan underground facility.
